title: Fermented Glassy-Jelly Russian Rhubarb Braised Meat
description: A show-off Russian dish featuring forced-rhubarb, braise, and ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly flavors.

ingredients.name: Pork Belly
ingredients.notes: Choose a chunk of pork belly with a layer of fat on top.

ingredients.name: Forced Rhubarb
ingredients.notes: Use fresh rhubarb and blanch it in boiling water for 2 minutes to remove the bitterness.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Use filtered water and keep an eye on the water level to prevent the mixture from drying out.

ingredients.name: Molasses
ingredients.notes: Dark molasses has a rich, earthy flavor that pairs well with rhubarb.

ingredients.name: Coriander Seeds
ingredients.notes: Fermentation with coriander seeds adds a unique flavor profile.

ingredients.name: Black Pepper
ingredients.notes: A pinch of black pepper can help balance the flavors.

ingredients.name: Dill Seeds
ingredients.notes: Dill seeds can add a fresh flavor to the fermetation process.

instructions: In a large pot, combine pork belly, forced rhubarb, water, molasses, coriander seeds, black pepper, and dill seeds.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to 160 C and let it simmer for 1 hour.
Transfer the pot to a warm place and let it ferment for 2-3 days or until the natural yeast on the rhubarb has fermented the liquid to a glassy-jelly state.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the pork belly from the pot and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing it thinly.
Skim off any excess fat from the surface of the liquid and let it cool to room temperature before refrigerating it.
Refrigerate the braising liquid for at least 24 hours before serving.
Serve the braised meat with the glassy-jelly sauce spooned over the top.
Garnish with fresh dill or chives for added flavor and presentation.
Let the dish sit at room temperature for 30 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Make sure to serve the dish in a shallow, wide bowl to allow the sauce to coat the meat evenly.
